# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| 1.0.x   | :white_check_mark: |
| < 1.0   | :x:                |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

We take the security of VisualSpider seriously. If you have discovered a security vulnerability, we appreciate your help in disclosing it to us in a responsible manner.

### How to Report

Please report security vulnerabilities by emailing the maintainer directly or by creating a security advisory on GitHub:

- **GitHub Security Advisory**: [Create Advisory](https://github.com/badhope/VisualSpider/security/advisories/new)
- **Email**: badhope@users.noreply.github.com

### What to Include

Please include the following information in your report:

- Type of vulnerability
- Full path of source file(s) related to the vulnerability
- Any special configuration required to reproduce the issue
- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
- Proof-of-concept or exploit code (if possible)
- Impact of the vulnerability

### Response Timeline

- We will acknowledge your email within 48 hours
- We will send a more detailed response within 96 hours indicating the next steps
- We will keep you informed of the progress towards a fix

### Disclosure Policy

- Please do not disclose the vulnerability publicly until a fix has been released
- We will credit you for the discovery (unless you prefer to remain anonymous)

## Security Best Practices

When using VisualSpider:

- Only run the application with administrator privileges when necessary
- Be cautious when modifying system settings through the application
- Keep your system and the application up to date
- Review changes before applying them

Thank you for helping keep VisualSpider and its users safe!
